rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Flowserve Us, Inc. As Successor-In-Interest To Durametallic Corporations
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 15, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Flowserve US, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN J MOREHEAD et al VS. OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 15, 2015 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendants Lowe's Companies, Inc. and Lowe's Home Centers, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' second cause of action for products liability - survival is granted. As to summary judgment, pursuant to the three step governmental interest analysis, Virginia's Statute of Repose (VA Code Ann. § 8.01-250) applies. Plaintiffs concede that Virginia law applies to this issue. However, Virginia's Statute of Repose does not bar plaintiffs' claims. At a minimum, there is a triable issue whether the joint compound at issue to which Mr. Morehead was exposed was incorporated into an improvement to real property. This pertains to both the exposures occurring from the sanding of joint compound that had previously been applied to the wall as well as the cleaning up of joint compound that had previously fallen to the floor without ever having been applied to a wall. As to summary adjudication of plaintiffs' second cause of action for products liability, pursuant to the three-step governmental interest analysis, Virginia law applies. First, the laws of Virginia and California differ with respect to strict liability, a fact plaintiffs do not dispute. Second, in examining each jurisdiction's interest in the application of its own law under the circumstances of the particular case to determine whether a true conflict exists, there is not a true conflict as Virginia has an interest in the application of its law while California does not. The Court does not get to the third step of the analysis however, even if there were a true conflict, Virginia's interest would be more impaired if its policy were subordinated to the policy of California. Overall, defendants' alleged supply of asbestos-containing joint compound occurred in Virginia; decedent's exposure to this joint compound occurred in Virginia; the exposure occurred while decedent was a Virginia resident; and decedent was a Virginia resident at the time he was diagnosed with his asbestos-related illness(es). Thus plaintiffs' second cause of action is barred pursuant to Virginia law. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
